text.title: java-spring-boot Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring-boot as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Controllers placing business logic directly in the handler method or returning JPA entities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in controllers violates separation of concerns and makes unit testing harder. Returning entities causes LazyInitializationException and leaks internal state.

failure-mode.description: Placing business logic in controllers instead of services or calling private transactional methods via this.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Services are the proper encapsulation layer for business rules and transactional boundaries. Private method calls bypass the proxy.

failure-mode.description: Using the same class for both entity and DTO or passing raw entities through API boundaries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Entities should never be exposed over HTTP. The API contract (DTO) must be independent of the internal persistence model.

failure-mode.description: Returning custom JSON shapes for errors or allowing exceptions to propagate uncaught to the client.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Custom error shapes fragment the API contract. Uncaught exceptions expose stack traces and internal details. RFC 7807 ProblemDetail is the standardized format.

failure-mode.description: Iterating over a collection and accessing lazy relationships: for (User u : users) { System.out.println(u.getOrganization().getName()); }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each getOrganization() triggers a SELECT. For N users, N+1 queries are issued (one to fetch users, N to fetch each organization).
